journal_entry.reason: Delivery vehicle is acquired as a depreciable asset for $40,000. Payment is split: $20,000 (50%) paid immediately in cash, and $20,000 (50%) financed through a 2-month promissory note. The vehicle account is debited for the full acquisition cost. Cash is credited for the amount paid, and Notes Payable is credited for the financed portion.

resolved_ambiguities.resolution: Payment method (cash, check, wire) does not affect the journal entry structure — all reduce cash/bank account
Short-term note (2 months) — if interest not stated, convention is to record at face value; interest will be recorded when paid or at period-end if material
Vehicle specifications do not change the accounting entry — all are recorded as delivery vehicle/equipment asset

output.asset_increase_reason: Delivery vehicle (equipment) is acquired for $40,000, representing one asset increase. The vehicle is recorded at its full acquisition cost regardless of the payment method (cash + note).
output.dividend_increase_reason: No owner withdrawals or dividends in this transaction.
output.expense_increase_reason: Vehicle is a capital asset, not an expense. It will be depreciated over its useful life.
output.liability_decrease_reason: No liabilities are being paid off or reduced. The promissory note is being issued (liability increase on credit side), not decreased.
output.equity_decrease_reason: No equity accounts are reduced in this asset acquisition transaction.
output.revenue_decrease_reason: No revenue accounts are involved in this asset purchase transaction.

output.liability_increase_reason: The 2-month promissory note for $20,000 (50% of $40,000) creates a notes payable liability that increases.
output.equity_increase_reason: No equity accounts are affected; this is an asset acquisition financed by cash and debt.
output.revenue_increase_reason: No revenue is generated; this is a capital expenditure for a delivery vehicle asset.
output.asset_decrease_reason: Cash decreases by $20,000 (50% of the purchase price paid immediately).
output.dividend_decrease_reason: No owner withdrawals or dividends are involved in this transaction.
output.expense_decrease_reason: No expenses are reversed or decreased; the vehicle is capitalized as an asset, not expensed.

output.incomplete_information_reasoning: The transaction is clear. Atlas Van Lines purchased a delivery vehicle for $40,000, paying $20,000 (50%) in cash and issuing a 2-month promissory note for the remaining $20,000. This is a straightforward asset acquisition with mixed financing. No business facts are missing - the asset type, total cost, and payment terms are all specified.

debit_asset_increase.reason: Accept. One delivery vehicle acquired for $40,000 represents one asset increase.

debit_dividend_increase.reason: Accept. No dividend transaction in this purchase.

debit_expense_increase.reason: Accept. Vehicle purchase is capitalized as an asset, not expensed.

debit_liability_decrease.reason: Accept. No liability is being paid down in this transaction.

debit_revenue_decrease.reason: Accept. No revenue reversal in this transaction.

credit_liability_increase.reason: Accept. One promissory note issued for $20,000 represents one liability increase.

credit_equity_increase.reason: Accept. No equity contribution in this transaction.

credit_revenue_increase.reason: Accept. No revenue earned in this transaction.

credit_asset_decrease.reason: Accept. Cash paid out represents one asset decrease of $20,000.

credit_dividend_decrease.reason: Accept. No dividend reversal in this transaction.

credit_expense_decrease.reason: Accept. No expense reversal in this transaction.

entry.reason: Delivery vehicle is acquired as a depreciable asset for $40,000. Payment is split: $20,000 (50%) paid immediately in cash, and $20,000 (50%) financed through a 2-month promissory note. The vehicle account is debited for the full acquisition cost. Cash is credited for the amount paid, and Notes Payable is credited for the financed portion.

output.decision_rationale: The transaction is complete and unambiguous. The classifier tuples correctly identify: 1 debit for asset increase (delivery vehicle), 1 credit for asset decrease (cash payment), and 1 credit for liability increase (promissory note). The amounts are clearly stated: total cost $40,000, with 50% ($20,000) cash and 50% ($20,000) note. No tax is mentioned, so no tax lines are added. Total debits ($40,000) equal total credits ($20,000 + $20,000). The entry complies with IFRS standards for asset acquisition with mixed financing.
